visualizerParams.title: Cycle Detection with Kahn's Algorithm
visualizerParams.problemDescription: The provided cycle detection uses DFS. Refactor it to use Kahn's Algorithm (topological sort), which identifies cycles by checking if any nodes with incoming edges remain after the sorting process.

solution.explanation: Kahn's Algorithm is an iterative approach for cycle detection. If a graph is a DAG, we can visit all nodes by repeatedly removing those with zero in-degree. If any nodes remain unvisited, it implies a cycle exists that prevents their in-degrees from reaching zero.
